calendar.matters:
REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (538 Bryant St, San Francisco, Ca 94107)
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The Following matters were heard together: PTR-03-284369 The Jack Grillo Marital Trust & PTR-05-287573 The Virginia O. Grillo Living Trust.
Appearances: Mark Eptstein for (petitioner) Lloyd Crenna. George McNitt for the buyers.

Mr. Epstein stated that he filed a supplemental petition and declaration on Friday and that he has copies if the court wishes. The Court asked about P.C. 10309 and why an appraisal has not been filed. Mr. Epstein stated that attached to the document are consents and waivers, that they do not need a referee, that it is a trust property, that when the successor trustee was appointed Judge Dearman added into the order that court confirmation was needed for sale of property, that they had an appraisal in November 2015, that the contracts are for above Fair Market Value (FMV) and that they have met the confirmation process. Mr. McNitt stated that he agrees, that the orders appointing Mr. Crenna mention court confirmation and that the intent was to notify the beneficiaries of the sale. Mr. Epstein stated that all beneficiaries have consented and waived notice. The Court stated that it is not prepared to go forward today. Mr. McNitt stated that the sales contract had no notice, that nothing was filed until after escrow closed, that the buyer has been sitting, that court confirmation is not needed and that it is in the best interest of the trust and the beneficiaries to go forward today. Mr. Epstein stated that both trusts gave the trustee power to sell, that Judge Dearman included a line in each order, that FMV has been obtained, that there was an appraisal, that the property has been marketed, that it needs to close and that there has been no conflict of interest. The Court stated that it needed a bit of time to think about this and instructed the parties to stay around until the end of the calendar. The matter resumed and the Court stated that it was concerned with the policy that this could set. Mr. Epstein stated that there is a difference between a standard probate and a trust, that this is a trust situation, that the trustee has power of sale, that the code sections talk about the duties of the personal representative not a trustee, that when they came back into court to appoint the successor trustee Judge Dearman specified confirmation, that the trustee has met all of the requirements, that they had a comprehensive appraisal, that they had the best broker for marketing, that they obtained offers and accepted the highest offers, that the purchase prices are above the asking prices, that the trustee had forgotten about the confirmation process and that they want to avoid the buyers making a claim against the trust. The Court stated that it is not persuaded that the probate code does not apply, that the code still applies to the trustee but that it will allow the sale to go forward today. Re: 538 Bryant Street; The sale was confirmed at $1,490,000.00 (50% for each trust) based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt/review of the order). An additional bond amount is to be determined. Re: 566 Folsom Street; The sale was confirmed at $1,850,000.00 (50% for each trust) based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt/review of the order). An additional bond amount is to be determined.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
